channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: US defense chief says alliance with Philippines will transcend administrations
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin on Tuesday condemned China's dangerous actions against the Philippines and renewed a warning that the United States would defend its treaty ally if Filipino forces come under an armed attack in the increasingly volatile waters.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italian Premier Meloni's coalition suffers setbacks in 2 regional elections
item.description: Italy's Premier Giorgia Meloni suffered election setbacks in two regions as the opposition Democratic party won a tight race in the central Umbria region and deepened its control of the traditional left-leaning stronghold of Emilia-Romagna.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Takeaways from the AP's investigation into Osprey safety issues
item.description: The Osprey is back in the air after being grounded for months following a crash last November that killed eight U.S. service members in Japan.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Kevin Vineys And Aaron Kessler, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about Sean Duffy, Trump's choice to become transportation secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has chosen Sean Duffy to become transportation secretary in his new administration, positioning him to oversee a complex system that includes pipelines, railroads, cars, trucks, airlines and mass transit systems, as well as funding for highways.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Sean Duffy, Fox host and former congressman, for transportation secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump has picked Sean Duffy, a Fox Business host and former Republican congressman from Wisconsin, to head the Department of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: Trump picks Sean Duffy to be transportation secretary
item.description: Wisconsin Rep. Sean Duffy is a former reality TV star who was one of Trump’s most visible defenders on cable news.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, MICHELLE L. PRICE and DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia appeals court cancels arguments in Trump's bid to disqualify Fani Willis
item.description: A Georgia appeals court canceled next month’s oral arguments in President-elect Trump’s bid to disqualify Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis (D) from criminally prosecuting him. Monday's one-sentence order from the Georgia Court of Appeals canceling the Dec.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump nominates former Wisconsin Rep. Sean Duffy for Secretary of Transportation
item.description: President-elect Trump nominated former U.S. Rep. Sean Duffy to serve as the U.S. Secretary of Transportation on Monday, adding to his growing list of administration members.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump appears to be planning to attend SpaceX 'Starship' launch scheduled for Tuesday in Texas
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump appears to be planning to attend a SpaceX “Starship” rocket launch on Tuesday, in the latest indication of founder Elon Musk 's influence in the Republican's orbit.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump’s defense pick reignites the debate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled: Should women be allowed to serve their country by fighting on the front lines? The former Fox News commentator has made it clear, in his own b
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Republican Eric Hovde concedes defeat to Democrat Tammy Baldwin in US Senate race
item.description: Wisconsin Republican U.S. Senate candidate Eric Hovde has conceded defeat in the race narrowly won by Democratic Sen. Tammy Baldwin, saying he will not pursue a recount.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Some Arab Americans who voted for Trump are concerned about his picks for key positions
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Just a week after winning several of the nation’s largest Arab-majority cities, President-elect Donald Trump has filled top administration posts with staunch Israel supporters, including an ambassador to Israel who has claimed “there is no such thing as Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump's defense pick reignites debate
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: As China cracks down on bookstores at home, Chinese-language booksellers are flourishing overseas
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Yu Miao smiles as he stands among the 10,000 books crowded on rows of bamboo shelves in his newly reopened bookstore.
item.dc:creator: FU TING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Student protesters face potential Trump crackdown
item.description: Those on campus have already seen stricter rules around demonstrations this academic year, but President-elect Trump, as a candidate, has threatened protesters with everything from military action to deportation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pollster Ann Selzer steps aside in wake of huge Iowa poll miss
item.description: Top pollster Ann Selzer is set to retire from the Des Moines Register’s Iowa Poll after a shocking presidential survey was so wrong.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump taps FCC's Brendan Carr to lead the agency
item.description: Carr was seen as a pretty conventional Republican with a pro-corporate outlook for most of his career. More recently, he has embraced Trumpian themes about social media, tech and television companies.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Ron Johnson says his 'bias would be to vote' for Trump's picks
item.description: Sen. Ron Johnson, R-Wis., said he doesn't "particularly like being the human resource department of administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump allies remain in legal peril despite his election victory
item.description: President-elect Trump’s White House victory set his legal cases on a downward spiral, but his allies who have faced prosecution or other legal trouble are not yet in the clear.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names attorney Will Scharf to key White House job
item.description: Scharf represented Trump as one of his lawyers during a hearing on presidential immunity before the Supreme Court and was a regular on television defending Trump amid his hush money trial in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: 1 in 5 US residents 'more likely' to move after election, according to Redfin
item.description: Of those who say they are now more likely to move, 36% say the election results are making them consider a different country.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Women stock up on abortion pills and Plan B, fearing new restrictions under Trump
item.description: Telehealth providers say requests for the pills have spiked since the election. Patients and doctors worry what a Trump presidency could mean for medical abortion and emergency contraception.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden and Elissa Nadworny, MPR News

item.title: Hubbard County election judge faces felony charges for allegedly allowing unregistered voters to cast ballots
item.description: Prosecutors in Hubbard County say Timothy Michael Scouton — who was head judge at a precinct in rural Badoura Township — allowed 11 people to vote even though they had not registered.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Clark County finds 1,600 uncounted mail-in ballots in secure vault
item.description: Clark County elections officials discovered more than 1,600 uncounted mail-in ballots Thursday, more than a week after Election Day, a spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump picks former Georgia congressman for VA secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump has announced former Rep. Doug Collins (R-Ga.), a chaplain in the Air Force Reserve, as his pick for the next Secretary of Veterans Affairs.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: NJ Transit, Amtrak testify before New Jersey lawmakers
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - Some palpable tension could be felt during Thursday's Assembly Transportation and Independent Authorities (TIA) Committee, which featured NJ Transit President Kevin Corbett and Amtrak CEO Stephen Gardner.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: North Las Vegas city councilwoman wins re-election by 9 votes
item.description: North Las Vegas City Councilwoman Ruth Garcia-Anderson won re-election by 9 votes -- a difference of 0.04%, according to unofficial election results updated Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump picks Marco Rubio as his Secretary of State
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump named Sen. Marco Rubio of Florida as his nominee for secretary of state on Wednesday, setting up a onetime critic who evolved into one of the president-elect’s fiercest defenders to become the nation’s top diplomat.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump faces backlash to Hegseth pick for Pentagon chief
item.description: President-elect Trump’s surprise choice of Pete Hegseth, a conservative Fox News host, to serve as Defense secretary has enraged Democrats and left even some Republicans scratching their heads.
item.dc:creator: Colin Meyn, Fox 5

item.title: Trump draft executive order would set up board to oust generals en masse: Report
item.description: If signed by Trump once he takes office, it could allow the quick removal of generals and admirals and purge the ranks of those the future commander-in-chief takes issue with for whatever reason.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk responds after 'SNL' star claims he made her cry
item.description: In a now-deleted TikTok post, "Saturday Night Live" star Chloe Fineman said that Elon Musk made her cry when he hosted the show.
item.dc:creator: Darren Sweeney, Fox 5
